scenes.title: How an Interpreter Works
scenes.subtitle: Reading, understanding, and executing code one step at a time.
scenes.label: Scene 1 / 10
stepSubtitles: Presenting the Quick Bytes Interactive Interpreter Visualizer.
Executing instructions step-by-step at runtime.
Press play or next to begin the walkthrough.

scenes.title: Source Code
scenes.subtitle: A high-level script is written in languages like Python, JavaScript, or Ruby.
scenes.label: Scene 2 / 10
stepSubtitles: A programmer writes a Python script initializing variables and printing the sum.

scenes.title: The Big Idea
scenes.subtitle: Unlike a compiler, execution happens immediately during interpretation.
scenes.label: Scene 3 / 10
stepSubtitles: Input: Source code is read directly by the interpreter.
Process: The interpreter dynamically reads, parses, and executes statement by statement.
Output: Program prints results instantly to stdout console.
Execution Loop: Consists of continuous Read → Parse → Execute cycles.
Interactive VM: Code runs directly inside the environment with no standalone binaries generated.

scenes.title: Reading Line by Line
scenes.subtitle: The interpreter fetches the first statement, understands it, and records it in memory.
scenes.label: Scene 4 / 10
stepSubtitles: Reads statement 'x = 2', parses it, and creates key 'x' with value 2 in stack memory.

scenes.title: Continuing Execution
scenes.subtitle: It progresses to the next line, dynamically updating runtime values.
scenes.label: Scene 5 / 10
stepSubtitles: Reads statement 'y = 3', parses it, and creates key 'y' with value 3 in stack memory.

scenes.title: Evaluating Expressions
scenes.subtitle: Expressions are resolved by retrieving variables from the environment and executing functions.
scenes.label: Scene 6 / 10
stepSubtitles: Reads 'result = x + y' and fetches variable values from the environment stack.
Evaluates addition expression node: '2 + 3'.
Computes expression result: 5.
Saves variable 'result' with computed value 5 back into environment stack.

scenes.title: Producing Output
scenes.subtitle: System commands (like print) produce output instantly during interpretation.
scenes.label: Scene 7 / 10
stepSubtitles: Reads 'print(result)', fetches value of 'result' (5), and outputs to console.
The console prints 5 instantly to standard output.

scenes.title: How Execution Progresses
scenes.subtitle: A continuous loop of Read → Parse → Execute travels step-by-step through the program.
scenes.label: Scene 8 / 10
stepSubtitles: Step 1: Execute variable assignment 'x = 2' (Instruction type: Assign).
Step 2: Execute variable assignment 'y = 3' (Instruction type: Assign).
Step 3: Execute expression addition 'result = x + y' (Instruction type: Arithmetic).
Step 4: Execute system print 'print(result)' (Instruction type: Print).

scenes.title: Error Handling
scenes.subtitle: Interpreters stop execution immediately upon encountering the first runtime error.
scenes.label: Scene 9 / 10
stepSubtitles: Traceback error: Interpreter encounters undefined variable 'z' on line 3 and halts execution.

scenes.title: Final Summary
scenes.subtitle: An interpreter executes a program incrementally—reading, understanding, and running code one statement at a time.
scenes.label: Scene 10 / 10

codeString:
x = 2
y = 3
result = x + y
print(result)
